highlight.title: MCP Security Crisis Emerges with 30 CVEs in 60 Days
highlight.summary: <cite index="25-5,26-2,26-16">Analysis reveals that MCP has become AI's fastest-growing attack surface, with 30 CVEs filed in just 60 days, with the most popular trusted model context protocol servers containing severe cybersecurity vulnerabilities</cite>. <cite index="21-9,21-10">Organizations rushing to connect their LLM-powered apps to external data sources using MCP may be inadvertently creating attack surfaces fundamentally different from anything their existing security controls can handle</cite>.

resources.title: The 2026 MCP Roadmap
resources.why_now: <cite index="16-4,16-8">MCP now runs in production at companies large and small, powers agent workflows, and is shaped by a growing community through Working Groups, with core maintainers narrowing priorities down to the areas that matter most for 2026</cite>.

resources.title: OpenAI and Microsoft Support Model Context Protocol
resources.why_now: <cite index="14-4,14-10">OpenAI and Microsoft have become the latest organizations to throw their support behind MCP, with Microsoft releasing Playwright-MCP that enables AI agents to interact with web pages instead of simply answering questions about them</cite>.

resources.title: MCP Security in 2026: Lessons From Real Exploits
resources.why_now: <cite index="23-1,25-6">MCP became the standard for AI tool integration in 2026, but GitHub token leaks, RCE bugs, and supply chain attacks expose serious risks, with data showing that 38% of 500+ scanned servers completely lack authentication</cite>.

resources.title: Why MCP is Suddenly on Every Executive Agenda
resources.why_now: <cite index="8-10,8-12,8-13">MCP is emerging as a connective infrastructure for enterprise AI, with its sudden relevance coinciding with the rise of agentic AI, addressing both data retrieval needs and standardized methods for AI models to act on behalf of users</cite>.

claude_take: <cite index="19-1,11-1,8-21">What began as an experimental protocol has become the de facto integration layer for agentic AI - natively supported by major AI providers and deployed across millions of daily active developer tool users as of early 2026, with broad vendor standardization establishing MCP as the core integration interface across AI-native ecosystems</cite>. <cite index="8-21,22-10">MCP is becoming a control layer for how AI systems interact with enterprise environments, with the protocol's maintainers acknowledging that production readiness for organizations isn't optional anymore—it's the next phase</cite>.
